blueprints.title: Investigate an incident or production issue safely
blueprints.goal: Investigate a production issue, deploy problem, degraded service, or incident report by establishing impact, gathering runtime evidence, separating symptom from cause, and proposing the safest next step.

preconditions.description: There is a real or suspected operational issue affecting users, systems, deploys, or runtime behavior.
Some runtime evidence is available or can be collected, such as logs, alerts, metrics, rollout state, health checks, or environment status.

steps.title: Capture the incident scope
steps.instruction: State what is happening operationally: what broke, when it started, who or what is affected, and whether the issue is active, intermittent, or already mitigated. Distinguish observed impact from assumptions.

steps.title: Check current runtime and rollout state
steps.instruction: Inspect the live state first: deployment status, service health, recent releases, traffic behavior, process state, or environment differences. Confirm whether the system is still failing and under what conditions.
expectedEvidence: recent rollout or environment state reviewed

steps.title: Gather runtime evidence
steps.instruction: Collect the strongest available evidence from logs, metrics, alerts, traces, health checks, error reports, or state transitions. Prefer evidence that narrows timing, affected components, and failure boundaries.

steps.title: Separate symptom from probable cause
steps.instruction: Identify which observations are direct symptoms and which ones may point toward an underlying cause. Avoid treating downstream errors, user-visible effects, or mitigation side effects as the root cause without stronger evidence.

steps.title: Assess blast radius and urgency
steps.instruction: Estimate the blast radius: affected users, services, environments, regions, workflows, or data paths. Note whether the safest next step is observation, containment, rollback consideration, or deeper diagnosis.

steps.title: Recommend the safest next action
steps.instruction: Recommend the smallest safe next action based on evidence: continue investigation, apply containment, verify a rollback target, pause rollout, or gather one more decisive signal. If action is risky or user-visible, make that explicit.

steps.title: Report incident summary
steps.instruction: Summarize impact, current state, strongest evidence, leading hypothesis if any, blast radius, and the recommended next action. Make uncertainty explicit where evidence is incomplete.

branches.condition: If runtime evidence is sparse, keep the summary explicit about uncertainty and prioritize gathering one decisive operational signal before recommending a risky action.
If rollback is being considered, verify the exact target and expected blast-radius reduction before recommending it as the next action.

expectedEvidence.description: The current incident scope and affected surface were established from observed behavior.
Runtime evidence such as logs, metrics, rollout state, or health checks was reviewed.
Blast radius and urgency were assessed before recommending action.
The final answer reports impact, strongest evidence, uncertainty, and the safest next step.

failureModes.title: Jumping straight to rollback or fix without state verification
signals: rollback is suggested before checking current rollout state
runtime health is not inspected first
failureModes.mitigation: Verify the live state, active rollout condition, and likely blast-radius effect before recommending rollback or containment.

failureModes.title: Confusing symptoms with root cause
signals: user-visible failures are treated as the cause
downstream errors are labeled root cause without upstream evidence
failureModes.mitigation: Separate direct symptoms from the leading causal chain and keep uncertain hypotheses explicitly provisional.

failureModes.title: Ignoring blast radius
signals: analysis never states who or what is affected
next action is proposed without urgency or impact context
failureModes.mitigation: Assess the affected users, systems, environments, and workflows before recommending the next move.

failureModes.title: Treating sparse evidence as certainty
signals: conclusion sounds definitive despite missing runtime signals
failureModes.mitigation: Name uncertainty explicitly and recommend the next decisive signal when evidence is incomplete.
